text: Photosynthesis converts light energy to chemical energy (glucose) using CO₂ and H₂O, releasing O₂. Two stages: light-dependent reactions (thylakoid membrane, produce ATP + NADPH) and Calvin cycle (stroma, fix CO₂ into sugar). C₃ plants (most), C₄ plants (corn, tropical), CAM plants (succulents) differ in carbon fixation.
Calvin cycle: RuBisCO enzyme fixes CO₂ (most abundant enzyme on Earth).
Photosynthesis produces essentially all atmospheric oxygen.
